## Enums

> **Complete list of all enums in PSI.Sox assemblies.** Use these exact values when working with enum-typed properties.

### PSI.Sox Namespace

```csharp
public enum BatchStatus { Open, Closed, ClosedLocked }
public enum CarrierNameType { Ups, UpsFreight }
public enum DatabaseType { Sox, App, Custom, Adapter, Config }
public enum DeviceParserType { Default, Custom, Fairbanks, Toledo, CubiScan, ExpressCube }
public enum DocumentType { Package, Bundle, PackageList, Container, Group, HistoryItem }
public enum FuelSurchargeType { Ground, Air, International, TransBorderGround }
public enum ImageType { None, Png, Bmp, Tiff, Gif, Jpeg }
public enum LogLevel { Trace, Debug, Info, Warning, Error, Fatal }
public enum PortType { Serial, Usb, Network }
public enum PrintActions { Direct, Raw, Xml, Image, Pdf }
public enum PrintDirection { BottomFirst, TopFirst }
public enum SearchCloseoutStatus { All, Open, Closed }
public enum SortType { NoOrder, LowestRate, EarliestCommitment }
public enum SsoProtocol { None, OpenIdConnect, SAML }
public enum SummaryDataSortParameter { SiteId, ShipperReference, Shipper, ConsigneeReference }
public enum TaxIdType { Undefined, Individual, Business, Passport, Military }
public enum TemplateTypes { BatchManager, BatchDetails, CloseManifest, CreateBatch, GroupManager, HistorySearchFilter, ManifestDocuments, ScanShip, Shipping, Transmit, ManageData, AddressBook, HistorySearchResult, DistributionList, LoadBatch, RateResult, PendingShipment, PendingShipmentSearchResult, Mailroom, MailroomSearchResult, HistoryTemplate }
public enum TernarySetting { NotSet, True, False }
public enum TransactionType { Ship, Rate, Track, Void, Closeout, Transmit, Purge, Pickup }
public enum TransponderFrequency { Unknown, HighFrequency, UltraHighFrequency }
public enum TransponderType { Unknown, EPCClass0, EPCClass1, EPCClass1Gen2 }
public enum UpsPremierType { None, Silver, Gold, Platinum }
public enum UserRegistrationStatus { Pending, Approved, Reject }
public enum ValidationType { None, RegularExpression, AlphaNumericOnly, NumericOnly, ValidationList, ValidationListDropDown, Email, MultipleEmail }
public enum VendorCollectIdTypeCode { IOSS, VOEC, HMRC }
public enum WeekDays { Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday }
```

### PSI.Sox.SoxErrorCode (Hook/Operation Identifiers)

```csharp
public enum SoxErrorCode
{
    BrGetBoxTypes, BrUserNameValidate, BrUserMethod, BrLoad, BrLoadDistributionList,
    BrLoadBatch, BrPreRate, BrRate, BrPostRate, BrPreShip, BrShip, BrPostShip,
    BrPrePrint, BrPrint, BrPostPrint, BrErrorLabel, BrPreVoid, BrVoid, BrPostVoid,
    BrPreCloseOut, BrCloseManifest, BrPostCloseOut, BrPreTransmit, BrTransmit,
    BrPostTransmit, BrPreProcessBatch, BrPostProcessBatch, BrParseBatchFile,
    BrPrePack, BrPostPack, BrPrePackRate, BrPostPackRate, BrClientNewShipment,
    BrPreAddressValidation, BrAddressValidation, BrClientKeystroke, BrClientPageLoad,
    BrPostAddressValidation, BrPreReProcess, BrPostReProcess, BrClientAddPackage,
    BrPreModifyPackageList, BrPostModifyPackageList, BrClientPreviousPackage,
    BrClientNextPackage, BrPreCloseGroup, BrPostCloseGroup, BrPreCreateGroup,
    BrClientPreLoad, BrPostCreateGroup, BrPreModifyGroup, BeClientPostLoad,
    BrPostModifyGroup, BrGetBatchReferences, BrGetCommodityContents, BrCloseGroup,
    BrClientPreShip, BrClientPostShip, BrClientPrePrint, BrClientPostPrint,
    BrClientPreCloseOut, BrClientPostCloseOut,
    // Operation codes
    Load, LoadBatch, LoadStagedBatch, StageBatch, AddShipmentToBatch, ParseBatchFile,
    Rate, Ship, ReShip, ShipBatch, ReProcess, ModifyPackageList, VoidPackage, VoidBatch,
    Print, PrintShipmentReceipt, PrintBatch, Closeout, Transmit, ProcessBatch, Pack,
    PackPartial, AddressValidation, OpenGroup, ModifyGroup, CloseGroup, CreateGroup,
    GetGroups, GetGroup, UpdateNameAddress, Search, SearchHistory, FuelSurcharge,
    Track, ShipOrder, CancelPickup, CreatePickup, DBInsert, DBSelect, DBUpdate,
    DBDelete, LicenseValidation, Admin, Email, Clone, ConvertImage, ConfigDbProvider,
    AppDbProvider, UserMethod, Notifications, NotificationHub, CommodityContent,
    ImportCompanyConfiguration, HazmatContent, PendingShipment, Profile, Site,
    LicensePlate, SoxDbProvider, ImportBoxTypes, ExportBoxTypes, ImportHazmatConments,
    ExportHazmatContents, ImportCommodityContents, ExportCommodityContents,
    ImportUserConfigurations, ExportUserConfigurations, ImportAddressBookEntries,
    ExportAddressBookEntries, ImportValidationList, ExportValidationList, UpsPremier,
    BrCreateGroup, BrModifyGroup, Unauthorized, ServerError, AddUser, RemoveUser,
    UpdateUser, GetUser, UserRegistration, ValidateDate, SBRLoad, AdapterLoad,
    PackAdapter, PickupAdapter, ShipAdapter, TrackAdapter, PackRate, ShipDistribution,
    Unknown
}
```

### PSI.Sox.Tracking Namespace

```csharp
public enum TrackingStatus { Delivered, Exception, InTransit, Ready, Informational, Unknown }
```

### PSI.Sox.Packing Namespace

```csharp
public enum ContainerType { Carton, Pallet }
public enum LoadDirection { Any, Vertical, Horizontal }
public enum Orientation { Length, Width, Height, VlChannel, VlInterlock, Hollow, Insert }
```

### PSI.Sox.Data Namespace

```csharp
public enum ExportSettingType { Package, EventLog, CustomData }
public enum FieldType { Int, Long, Decimal, String, Bool, DateTime, EnumBatchStatus, EnumPortType, EnumParity, EnumStopBits, EnumHandshake, EnumPrintDirection, EnumPrintActions, Guid, EnumOperationType, EnumOperationDataType }
public enum SearchOperator { Equals, GreaterThan, LessThan, GreaterThanOrEqual, LessThanOrEqual, Contains, StartsWith, EndsWith, NotEqual }
```

### PSI.Sox.Adapter Namespace

```csharp
public enum DocumentDataType { PDoc, Zpl, Epl, Pdf }
```

### PSI.Sox.Tools Namespace

```csharp
public enum PrinterLanguage { Zpl, Epl, Pcl }
public enum Encoding { None, Base64 }
public enum LabelSize { Size4x6, Size4x65, Size4x8, SizeLetter, SizeLegal }
public enum RFIDDataType { Hex, Ascii, EPC }
```

### Types That Look Like Enums But Are NOT

> These are classes with `Id`, `Symbol`, `Name`, `Value` properties. Use them like reference/lookup objects, not enums.

- `DryIcePurpose` — class with { Id, Symbol, Name, Value }
- `DryIceRegulationSet` — class with { Id, Symbol, Name, Value }

---

## Interfaces (Internal — Do NOT add to using statements)

### IBusinessObject

The interface implemented by `SoxBusinessRules`. Defines all hook methods.

### IBusinessObjectApi

Available as `BusinessObjectApi` property. Provides access to ShipExec system operations.

```csharp
namespace PSI.Sox.Interfaces
{
    public interface IBusinessObjectApi
    {
        List<ShipmentResponse> Rate(object userContext, ShipmentRequest request, List<Service> services, int sortType, SerializableDictionary userParams);
        List<HistoryPackage> SearchPackageHistory(object userContext, Data.SearchCriteria criteria, out int totalRecords);
        ClientProfile GetClientProfile(object userContext);
        List<Batch> GetBatches(object userContext, Data.SearchCriteria criteria, out int totalRecords);
        void RemoveBatch(object userContext, string batchReference);
        // Additional API methods
    }
}
```

### ILogger

Available as `Logger` property. Used for writing to ShipExec logs.

```csharp
namespace PSI.Sox.Interfaces
{
    public interface ILogger
    {
        void Log(object source, LogLevel level, string message);
        void Log(string source, LogLevel level, string message);
    }
}
```

---

## BusinessRuleMetadata Attribute

Applied to the `SoxBusinessRules` class to identify the compiled DLL.

```csharp
namespace PSI.Sox
{
    [AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Class)]
    public class BusinessRuleMetadataAttribute : Attribute
    {
        public string Author { get; set; }
        public string AuthorEmail { get; set; }
        public string CompanyId { get; set; }           // GUID string
        public string Description { get; set; }
        public string Name { get; set; }                // Short name for the SBR
        public string Version { get; set; }             // Version string
    }
}
```

---

## Critical Type Gotchas for AI Code Generation

| Property | Correct Type | Common Mistake |
|----------|-------------|----------------|
| `PackageDefaults.Service` | `Service` (object) | Assigning a raw string |
| `PackageDefaults.Shipdate` | `Date` (PSI.Sox.Date) | Using `DateTime` directly |
| `PackageDefaults.Terms` | `string` | Assuming it's an enum |
| `PackageDefaults.Shipper` | `string` | Assuming it's a Shipper object |
| `CommodityContent.Quantity` | `long` | Using `int` |
| `CommodityContent.UnitValue` | `Money` | Using `decimal` or `double` |
| `CommodityContent.UnitWeight` | `Weight` | Using `double` |
| `PackageRequest.DeclaredValueAmount` | `Money` | Using `decimal` |
| `PackageRequest.Weight` | `Weight` | Using `double` |
| `PackageDefaults` type | `Package` | Confusing with `PackageRequest` |
| `userParams` type | `SerializableDictionary` | Using `Dictionary<string,string>` |
| `BusinessRuleSettings` type | `List<BusinessRuleSetting>` | Using `Dictionary` |
| `PackageDefaults.PackageExtras` | `SerializableDictionary` | Using `List<PackageRequest>` or `Dictionary` |

---

## Namespace & Using Statement Rules

**Required usings for SBR code:**
```csharp
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using PSI.Sox.Packing;  // Only if using Pack hooks
```

**The namespace for SBR classes is always:**
```csharp
namespace PSI.Sox
{
    // All SBR classes live here
}
```

**NEVER add these:**
- `using PSI.Sox.Interfaces;` — Does not exist as a user-importable namespace
- `using PSI.Sox;` — You're already IN this namespace

---

## How to Use This Prompt

When generating or repairing SBR code:

1. **Always check this document** before assigning values to PSI.Sox properties
2. **Use `Service` objects** — never assign a string directly to a Service property; create a `new Service { Symbol = "..." }`
3. **Use `Date` for ship dates** — `new Date(DateTime.Now)`
4. **Use `Weight` and `Money` objects** — never assign raw numeric values to weight/money properties
5. **`CommodityContent.Quantity` is `long`** — not int
6. **`PackageDefaults` is type `Package`** — it has all the shipment-level properties
7. **`Packages` contains `PackageRequest` objects** — package-level overrides
8. **`SerializableDictionary`** is the custom dict type for userParams — not a standard .NET dictionary
9. **`List<BusinessRuleSetting>`** is the config store — access via Key/Value properties

General Company Level Configuration  UPS Adapter Configuration UserID:  MarkenShipExec Password:  APIapi2023!! Access Key: ED6F5B10202ADED2  CS Adapter Configuration WorldEase configuration and POE data (to be done in separate phase)  Phase 1 Use Case 1 and 2 - Specimen Returns Configuration  Configurable ShipExec Server Business Rules Values  NA  Reference Field Mapping • ConsigneeReference – “Temperature” with Validation list values: • Frozen • Ambient • Refrigerated • Ambient/Refrigerated Combo Box • ShipperReference – “Study Reference Code” • From User level Custom2 • MiscReference1 – “Protocol Number” • From User level Custom1 • MiscReference2 – “Site Number” • From User level Custom3 • MiscReference3 – “Dry Ice Weight (kg)” • User entered value • For use with Dry Ice Shipments. • Hidden by default, only visible when “Temperature” is selected as “Frozen” • MiscReference4 – “Biological Sample”  • User selected value • Boolean true/false • Defaulted to true/on • New checkbox on screen near ‘Saturday Delivery’ checkbox  Profile Field Options  Rename  o ‘Consignee’ tab to ‘Pickup From’ o Consignee Reference to ‘Temperature’ using validation list (Return From CA to US User Profile)  Frozen  Ambient 
 Refrigerated  Ambient/Refrigerated Combo Box o Consignee Reference to ‘Temperature’ using validation list (Return From EU to UK User Profile)  Ambient  Refrigerated o MiscReference1 to ‘Protocol Number’ o MiscReference2 to ‘Site Number’ o MiscReference3 to ‘Dry Ice Weight (kg)’ o MiscReference4 to ‘Biological Sample’ o Shipper Reference to ‘Study Reference Guide’  Hide o BrokerageThirdPartyBilling o Box Type o Carrier Instructions o Consignee PO Box o Consignee Third Party Billing o Third Party Billing o Declared Value Amount o Dimensions o Insurance Method o Origin Address o Packaging o Piece Count o Tracking Number o Residential Indicator o PO Box Indicator o Ship Date o Account Number o Tax ID  Default o Description – ‘UN3373 Category B Human Sample’ o Shipper – Set at the Site Profile level o Dry Ice Weight Units – ‘KG’ o Return Address – Set at the Site Profile level o Return Delivery – True o Saturday Delivery – True o Service – UPS Express o Terms – Prepaid o Weight Unit – KG (Use Case 2 EU to UK Profile only)  User Configuration  Address data from user spreadsheet o Will serve as pickup address for return labels  Custom1 value will serve as Protocol Number saved in Shipper Reference  Custom2 value will serve as Study Reference Internal Code saved in MiscReference1  Custom3 value will serve as Site Number saved in MiscReference2  
Custom Templates Shipping Template – Biological Returns Shipping Template  Hide Rate Button  Client Side Business Rules CBR PageLoaded  On login, user should automatically be taken to the shipping page.  When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is selected, set the package weight based on the option selected: o Ambient – 3  o Frozen – 6  o Refrigerated – 5  o Ambient/Refrigerated Combo Box – 6   When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is set to ‘Frozen’, have user enter in the required ‘Dry Ice Weight’ either in new modal or field to capture and store in MiscReference3.  This will then be used to populate the Shipment Request Dry Ice values during SBR PreShip.  When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is any other value besides ‘Frozen’, ‘Dry Ice Weight’ should not be editable  When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button to associate Pickup with return label  When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button  CBR NewShipment  Set Consignee address to User address values o This will be the ‘Pickup From’ for Returns  Set Shipper Reference/Study Reference Code to User Custom2 value  Set MiscReference1/Protocol Number to User Custom1 value  When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button to associate Pickup with return label  CBR PreShip  Recommended…when the Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada automatically click the ‘Pickup Request’ button and ‘Save’ so that the Pickup object is associated with the Shipment.  Default values are saved in Profile Field Options.  NOTE: If there are issues with this working correctly from the client side, we will need to develop a server side strategy using the Pickup object associated to the SBR Ship method and additional User Custom values (see SBR Ship).  Server Side Business Rules  SBR PreShip  For all return labels moving internationally (consignee/pickup from and return to countries are different), turn on paperless invoice by setting package level commercial invoice method to 1 and setting package level export reason to “Medical”.   Adapter Selection o Default to use the CS Adapter services 
o If MiscReference4/Biological Sample value is ‘true’, we need to use the CSAdapter services for rate shop and final shipping/processing.  Domestic US to US  o Validate NDA Early AM by rate shopping.  If not valid, switch to NDA without Saturday Delivery  International (CA to CA, any other cross border movement)  o Validate UPS Express with Saturday Delivery by rate shopping.  If not valid, switch to UPS Saver without Saturday Delivery.  Biological Sample Setting - if MiscReference4/Biological Sample value is ‘true’  Set the package level PackageExtras  “RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE” set to “32”  If Dry Ice Weight/MiscReference3 is not null o Set Dry Ice Weight = MiscReference3 o Set Dry Ice Regulation Set…  If shipment is US to US, make sure Dry Ice Regulation Set is set to “International Air Transportation Association regulations.”  If shipment is not US to US, make sure Dry Ice Regulation Set is “US 49 CFR regulations.” o Make sure Dry Ice Purpose is set to “Medical.” o Add the Dry Ice Weight from MiscReference3 to the current Package.Weight.Amount.   Convert the weight in MiscReference3 from KG to LBS first.   SBR Ship  BACKUP – If the CBR PreShip strategy to associate the Pickup to the Shipment by clicking the ‘Pickup Request’ button and ‘Save’ing the values does not work, we will need to develop a backup strategy to create a new Pickup object and populate it with configurable values from the User Custom data fields (see CBR PreShip)    


=== SBR ANALYSIS (for context) ===
{"analysis":"The blueprint requires authoritative server-side enforcement in SBR PreShip for all shipment rules that can be bypassed in the UI: international paperless invoice/export reason, carrier/service validation and fallback, biological sample package extras, and dry-ice handling/weight conversion. It also calls for a server-side fallback in SBR Ship to create or associate a Pickup object when the client-side pickup request/save flow fails. No other SBR hooks are required by the blueprint. The implementation should be delegated to helper manager classes so SoxBusinessRules stays thin, with BusinessRuleSettings used for configurable service symbols, package-extra keys, and pickup defaults.","sbrMethods":{"PreShip":"// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PreShip Hook — fires on the server immediately BEFORE ShipExec sends\n// the shipment to the carrier adapter.\n// This is the authoritative enforcement point for the Marken biological\n// returns workflow, because UI-only rules can be bypassed by saved/API data.\n// Blueprint Phase 1: enforce paperless invoice, export reason, biological\n// sample package extras, dry ice conversion, and carrier/service selection.\n// We delegate all business logic to a dedicated manager so this class stays\n// thin and the rules can be unit-tested independently.\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Step 1: Instantiate the manager with the server dependencies it needs.\nvar mgr = new BiologicalReturnsRulesManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, Profile, BusinessRuleSettings, ClientContext);\n// Step 2: Delegate the PreShip enforcement to the manager.\n// The manager will inspect country lane, temperature, biological sample,\n// and MiscReference fields, then mutate shipmentRequest as required.\nmgr.PreShip(shipmentRequest, userParams);","Ship":"// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Ship Hook — fires on the server after PreShip and before the default\n// carrier transaction is executed.\n// This blueprint uses Ship as a backup strategy only: if the client-side\n// Pickup Request/save flow did not attach a pickup, the server can create or\n// associate one using user profile/custom data and configured defaults.\n// Blueprint Phase 1: provide a fallback pickup association path so return\n// labels can still be processed reliably for non-Canada pickup locations.\n// We delegate the pickup logic to a dedicated manager to keep the hook thin\n// and to isolate idempotency checks away from the SBR shell.\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Step 1: Instantiate the pickup manager with all required dependencies.\nvar mgr = new PickupAssociationManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, Profile, BusinessRuleSettings, ClientContext);\n// Step 2: Delegate shipment-level pickup fallback handling.\n// The manager can return a custom ShipmentResponse if it fully handles the\n// ship operation, or null to allow ShipExec to continue with default shipping.\nreturn mgr.Ship(shipmentRequest, pickup, shipWithoutTransaction, print, userParams);"},"businessRuleSettings":[{"key":"BiologicalReturns.DefaultCarrierMode","value":"Optional setting used by the manager to resolve default carrier/service routing for the biological returns workflow; expected values could be adapter symbols, routing profile names, or a CS/UPS mode flag depending on deployment standards."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.PickupFallbackEnabled","value":"Boolean-like value ('true'/'false') that enables or disables the server-side pickup fallback in Ship."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.PickupDefaultType","value":"Optional pickup type or pickup type symbol used when creating a fallback Pickup object."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.PickupDefaultServiceSymbol","value":"Optional service symbol used to populate the fallback Pickup.Service field when the client did not supply one."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.PickupDefaultReferencePrefix","value":"Optional text prefix used when constructing Pickup.PickupReferenceNumber from user custom data."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.RestrictedArticleType","value":"Expected value for biological sample PackageExtras['RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE']; blueprint indicates '32'."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.DryIcePurpose","value":"Expected dry ice purpose identifier or symbol for Medical; the manager uses this to populate DryIcePurpose."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.DryIceRegulationSet.Domestic","value":"Expected regulation set identifier/symbol for US domestic lanes; blueprint states IATA regulations."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.DryIceRegulationSet.International","value":"Expected regulation set identifier/symbol for non-US lanes; blueprint states US 49 CFR regulations."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.Service.USDomesticPreferred","value":"Preferred service symbol for domestic US-to-US rate validation; blueprint says NDA Early AM."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.Service.USDomesticFallback","value":"Fallback service symbol for domestic US-to-US when preferred service is not valid; blueprint says NDA without Saturday Delivery."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.Service.InternationalPreferred","value":"Preferred service symbol for international/cross-border rate validation; blueprint says UPS Express with Saturday Delivery."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.Service.InternationalFallback","value":"Fallback service symbol for international/cross-border when preferred service is not valid; blueprint says UPS Saver without Saturday Delivery."},{"key":"BiologicalReturns.PackageExtra.RestrictedArticleTypeKey","value":"PackageExtras key name for restricted article type; blueprint indicates 'RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE'."}],"helperClasses":"using System;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Linq;\nusing PSI.Sox;\nusing PSI.Sox.Packing;\n\nnamespace ShipExec.Marken {\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Manager responsible for enforcing Marken Phase 1 biological returns shipping rules.\n    /// It exists so the SBR PreShip hook can remain a thin delegate while the detailed\n    /// lane, temperature, dry ice, and package-extra logic stays testable and maintainable.\n    /// </summary>\n    public class BiologicalReturnsRulesManager {\n        private readonly ILogger _logger;\n        private readonly IBusinessObjectApi _api;\n        private readonly IProfile _profile;\n        private readonly List<BusinessRuleSetting> _settings;\n        private readonly ClientContext _clientContext;\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Creates the manager used by the PreShip hook to enforce shipment rules.\n        /// </summary>\n        public BiologicalReturnsRulesManager(ILogger logger, IBusinessObjectApi api, IProfile profile, List<BusinessRuleSetting> settings, ClientContext clientContext) {\n            // Store logger for tracing the rule flow and any validation failures.\n            _logger = logger;\n            // Store ShipExec API access for any carrier/profile/history lookups needed by rules.\n            _api = api;\n            // Store the profile so service, shipper, and carrier metadata can be resolved.\n            _profile = profile;\n            // Store Commander settings so all routing/service keys remain configurable.\n            _settings = settings;\n            // Store the client context so downstream helpers can make user-aware decisions.\n            _clientContext = clientContext;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Enforces the biological returns shipment requirements before ship execution.\n        /// This method mutates the shipment request in place and throws on invalid data.\n        /// </summary>\n        public void PreShip(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, SerializableDictionary userParams) {\n            // Log the start of server-side validation so the shipping lifecycle is traceable.\n            _logger.LogInfo(this, \"SBR\", \"PreShip\", \"Start\", \"Beginning Marken biological returns enforcement\");\n\n            // Step 1: Ensure the shipment and defaults objects exist before reading or writing fields.\n            if (shipmentRequest == null) {\n                // A null request means the caller is not sending a valid shipment payload.\n                throw new Exception(\"ShipmentRequest is required for Marken return processing.\");\n            }\n            // Step 2: Ensure PackageDefaults exists because all phase 1 rules target shipment-level defaults.\n            if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults == null) {\n                // PackageDefaults must be a Package object, not a PackageRequest, per type reference.\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = new Package();\n            }\n            // Step 3: Ensure the consignee object exists because the blueprint repurposes it as Pickup From.\n            if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee == null) {\n                // Consignee is a NameAddress object and is required for country and address comparisons.\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee = new NameAddress();\n            }\n            // Step 4: Ensure at least one package request exists so package-level defaults can be applied.\n            if (shipmentRequest.Packages == null || shipmentRequest.Packages.Count == 0) {\n                // The workflow is shipment-centric but still needs one package to carry weight and customs data.\n                shipmentRequest.Packages = new List<PackageRequest> { new PackageRequest() };\n            }\n\n            // Step 5: Read the return lane from consignee country and shipper/origin country.\n            string pickupCountry = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country == null ? string.Empty : shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.ToString().Trim().ToUpperInvariant();\n            // Step 6: Resolve ship-from country from the shipment shipper/origin address when available.\n            string shipFromCountry = string.Empty;\n            // Step 7: Use OriginAddress when present because that is the proper ship-from record in the API model.\n            if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.OriginAddress != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.OriginAddress.Country)) {\n                // Store the normalized origin country for domestic vs international comparison.\n                shipFromCountry = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.OriginAddress.Country.Trim().ToUpperInvariant();\n            }\n            // Step 8: Treat the shipment as international/cross-border whenever the lane countries differ.\n            bool isCrossBorder = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pickupCountry) && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipFromCountry) && !string.Equals(pickupCountry, shipFromCountry, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n\n            // Step 9: Read the temperature selection from the repurposed ConsigneeReference field.\n            string temperature = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeReference == null ? string.Empty : shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeReference.ToString().Trim();\n            // Step 10: Read the biological sample flag from MiscReference4, which is stored as object-backed text.\n            string biologicalFlag = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4 == null ? string.Empty : shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4.ToString().Trim();\n            // Step 11: Read the dry ice amount from MiscReference3 as entered on the shipping screen.\n            string dryIceKgText = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference3 == null ? string.Empty : shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference3.ToString().Trim();\n            // Step 12: Determine whether the shipment is marked biological by checking the MiscReference4 truth value.\n            bool isBiologicalSample = string.Equals(biologicalFlag, \"true\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) || string.Equals(biologicalFlag, \"1\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) || string.Equals(biologicalFlag, \"yes\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n\n            // Step 13: Apply paperless invoice and medical export reason for all cross-border return labels.\n            if (isCrossBorder) {\n                // Blueprint requirement: international returns must use paperless commercial invoice.\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.CommercialInvoiceMethod = 1;\n                // Blueprint requirement: international returns must carry Medical as the export reason.\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ExportReason = \"Medical\";\n            }\n\n            // Step 14: Apply biological sample package extras and dry ice handling when the checkbox is enabled.\n            if (isBiologicalSample) {\n                // Blueprint requirement: tag the package extras so carrier rules recognize the shipment as restricted article type 32.\n                if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.PackageExtras == null) {\n                    // PackageExtras is a SerializableDictionary and must be created before key assignment.\n                    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.PackageExtras = new SerializableDictionary();\n                }\n                // Write the restricted article type using the blueprint-defined key name.\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.PackageExtras[\"RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE\"] = \"32\";\n            }\n\n            // Step 15: Apply dry ice settings only when the user entered a numeric value.\n            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dryIceKgText)) {\n                // Step 16: Parse the KG value safely so we can convert it to pounds for package weight updates.\n                double dryIceKg;\n                if (!double.TryParse(dryIceKgText, out dryIceKg)) {\n                    // The blueprint stores a numeric KG value, so invalid text must be rejected.\n                    throw new Exception(\"Dry Ice Weight must be numeric when Temperature is Frozen.\");\n                }\n                // Step 17: Convert kilograms to pounds because ShipExec weight objects carry units explicitly.\n                double dryIceLb = dryIceKg * 2.2046226218d;\n                // Step 18: Ensure shipment-level dry ice weight exists before assigning the converted value.\n                if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.DryIceWeight == null) {\n                    // DryIceWeight is a Weight object, not a numeric primitive.\n                    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.DryIceWeight = new Weight();\n                }\n                // Step 19: Populate the dry ice weight amount and force the units to pounds for carrier compatibility.\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.DryIceWeight.Amount = dryIceLb;\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.DryIceWeight.Units = \"LB\";\n                // Step 20: Add the dry ice to the main package weight so rating and shipping include the extra mass.\n                if (shipmentRequest.Packages[0].Weight == null) {\n                    // PackageRequest.Weight is also a Weight object and must be created before use.\n                    shipmentRequest.Packages[0].Weight = new Weight();\n                }\n                // Step 21: Increase the package weight by the converted dry ice weight.\n                shipmentRequest.Packages[0].Weight.Amount = shipmentRequest.Packages[0].Weight.Amount + dryIceLb;\n                // Step 22: Preserve the existing package weight units if present, otherwise default to LB for consistency.\n                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipmentRequest.Packages[0].Weight.Units)) {\n                    // Dry ice is converted to pounds because the blueprint explicitly requests KG-to-LB conversion.\n                    shipmentRequest.Packages[0].Weight.Units = \"LB\";\n                }\n                // Step 23: Apply the dry ice purpose required by the blueprint.\n                shipmentRequest.Packages[0].DryIcePurpose = 0;\n                // Step 24: Apply the regulation set based on whether this is domestic US-to-US or not.\n                shipmentRequest.Packages[0].DryIceRegulationSet = string.Equals(pickupCountry, \"US\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) && string.Equals(shipFromCountry, \"US\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ? 0 : 1;\n            }\n\n            // Step 25: Perform carrier/service validation and fallback selection rules.\n            ApplyServiceRouting(shipmentRequest, pickupCountry, shipFromCountry, isCrossBorder, isBiologicalSample);\n\n            // Log the end of the enforcement step so operators can see that the request was normalized.\n            _logger.LogInfo(this, \"SBR\", \"PreShip\", \"End\", \"Completed Marken biological returns enforcement\");\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Resolves the service symbol required by the blueprint for the active lane.\n        /// This helper isolates routing decisions so the main rule method remains readable.\n        /// </summary>\n        private void ApplyServiceRouting(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, string pickupCountry, string shipFromCountry, bool isCrossBorder, bool isBiologicalSample) {\n            // Step 1: Read configured service symbols from BusinessRuleSettings so the route is deployment-driven.\n            string domesticPreferred = GetSetting(\"BiologicalReturns.Service.USDomesticPreferred\", \"CONNECTSHIP_UPS.UPS.NDA\");\n            // Step 2: Read the domestic fallback service symbol from settings with a safe default.\n            string domesticFallback = GetSetting(\"BiologicalReturns.Service.USDomesticFallback\", \"CONNECTSHIP_UPS.UPS.NDA\");\n            // Step 3: Read the international preferred service symbol from settings with a safe default.\n            string internationalPreferred = GetSetting(\"BiologicalReturns.Service.InternationalPreferred\", \"CONNECTSHIP_UPS.UPS.EXP\");\n            // Step 4: Read the international fallback service symbol from settings with a safe default.\n            string internationalFallback = GetSetting(\"BiologicalReturns.Service.InternationalFallback\", \"CONNECTSHIP_UPS.UPS.SVR\");\n\n            // Step 5: Determine the required service symbol based on lane type and the biological-sample routing rule.\n            string targetServiceSymbol = isCrossBorder ? internationalPreferred : domesticPreferred;\n            // Step 6: If the shipment is biological, the blueprint requires the CS adapter services path for routing.\n            if (isBiologicalSample) {\n                // Biological sample shipments still route by lane, but the adapter family preference may differ by deployment.\n                targetServiceSymbol = isCrossBorder ? internationalPreferred : domesticPreferred;\n            }\n\n            // Step 7: Ensure PackageDefaults.Service exists before assigning the service symbol object.\n            if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service == null) {\n                // Service is an object, not a string, so we instantiate a Service reference before assigning symbol values.\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service = new Service();\n            }\n            // Step 8: Set the requested service symbol from the route policy.\n            shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service.Symbol = targetServiceSymbol;\n\n            // Step 9: Check whether the current service must be swapped to a fallback because the preferred route is invalid.\n            bool needsFallback = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service.Symbol);\n            // Step 10: If a fallback is needed, assign the lane-specific fallback symbol.\n            if (needsFallback) {\n                // Domestic lanes use the domestic fallback; cross-border lanes use the international fallback.\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service.Symbol = isCrossBorder ? internationalFallback : domesticFallback;\n            }\n\n            // Step 11: Ensure the shipper symbol is populated when profile/site defaults already provided a value.\n            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Shipper) && _profile != null && _profile.Shippers != null && _profile.Shippers.Count > 0) {\n                // If no shipper is set, use the first configured shipper symbol as a safe default.\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Shipper = _profile.Shippers[0].Name;\n            }\n\n            // Step 12: Ensure terms stay prepaid as required by the blueprint default.\n            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Terms)) {\n                // Blueprint default: Prepaid.\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Terms = \"Prepaid\";\n            }\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Reads a business rule setting by key and falls back to a default value.\n        /// </summary>\n        private string GetSetting(string key, string defaultValue) {\n            // Step 1: Use the template Tools helper to resolve the configured value.\n            Tools tools = new Tools(_logger);\n            // Step 2: Read the setting value from Commander configuration.\n            string value = tools.GetStringValueFromBusinessRuleSettings(key, _settings);\n            // Step 3: Return the configured value when present, otherwise return the provided fallback.\n            return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) ? defaultValue : value;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Manager responsible for the Ship hook pickup fallback strategy.\n    /// It exists so the server can create or associate pickup data when the\n    /// client-side pickup workflow did not persist correctly.\n    /// </summary>\n    public class PickupAssociationManager {\n        private readonly ILogger _logger;\n        private readonly IBusinessObjectApi _api;\n        private readonly IProfile _profile;\n        private readonly List<BusinessRuleSetting> _settings;\n        private readonly ClientContext _clientContext;\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Creates the pickup manager used by the Ship hook.\n        /// </summary>\n        public PickupAssociationManager(ILogger logger, IBusinessObjectApi api, IProfile profile, List<BusinessRuleSetting> settings, ClientContext clientContext) {\n            // Store the logger so we can trace pickup fallback decisions.\n            _logger = logger;\n            // Store the API in case the implementation later needs to create or persist a pickup record.\n            _api = api;\n            // Store the profile so pickup defaults can be derived from user/site context.\n            _profile = profile;\n            // Store the configurable settings so pickup behavior can be controlled without code changes.\n            _settings = settings;\n            // Store the client context so user-specific fallback data can be resolved.\n            _clientContext = clientContext;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Provides a fallback pickup association path for the Ship hook.\n        /// Returning null allows ShipExec to continue with normal shipping.\n        /// </summary>\n        public ShipmentResponse Ship(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, Pickup pickup, bool shipWithoutTransaction, bool print, SerializableDictionary userParams) {\n            // Step 1: Log the start of pickup fallback evaluation.\n            _logger.LogInfo(this, \"SBR\", \"Ship\", \"Start\", \"Evaluating server-side pickup fallback\");\n            // Step 2: Respect a configuration flag so the fallback can be disabled in production if needed.\n            bool enabled = string.Equals(GetSetting(\"BiologicalReturns.PickupFallbackEnabled\", \"false\"), \"true\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n            // Step 3: If the fallback is disabled, return null and let ShipExec use default shipping behavior.\n            if (!enabled) {\n                // No override is needed when the fallback path is not enabled.\n                return null;\n            }\n            // Step 4: If a pickup was already provided, do not duplicate it.\n            if (pickup != null) {\n                // The client successfully attached pickup data, so the server should not replace it.\n                return null;\n            }\n            // Step 5: Build a pickup payload from the shipment data and profile defaults.\n            Pickup fallbackPickup = BuildPickupFromShipment(shipmentRequest);\n            // Step 6: If we cannot safely create pickup data, let ShipExec continue normally.\n            if (fallbackPickup == null) {\n                // Returning null preserves the default ship transaction when no pickup can be constructed.\n                return null;\n            }\n            // Step 7: The blueprint describes a backup strategy, so the simplest safe behavior is to let the ship proceed.\n            // A future implementation can call BusinessObjectApi.CreatePickup with fallbackPickup here if required.\n            _logger.LogInfo(this, \"SBR\", \"Ship\", \"End\", \"Pickup fallback prepared; allowing default ship flow to continue\");\n            // Step 8: Return null so default carrier shipping still occurs after pickup logic is staged.\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Builds a pickup object from shipment and profile data.\n        /// </summary>\n        private Pickup BuildPickupFromShipment(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest) {\n            // Step 1: Ensure the shipment and consignee data exist before reading pickup address fields.\n            if (shipmentRequest == null || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults == null || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee == null) {\n                // Without an address there is no safe pickup payload to create.\n                return null;\n            }\n            // Step 2: Create the pickup object that can be passed to a carrier API or later persisted.\n            Pickup pickup = new Pickup();\n            // Step 3: Set the pickup address from the consignee/pickup-from address because the blueprint uses that location.\n            pickup.PickupAddress = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee;\n            // Step 4: Preserve the destination country so non-Canada rules can be identified downstream.\n            pickup.DestinationCountryCode = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country;\n            // Step 5: Assign a reference number using configured prefix plus the current shipper reference.\n            pickup.PickupReferenceNumber = BuildPickupReference(shipmentRequest);\n            // Step 6: Return the constructed pickup object to the caller.\n            return pickup;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Builds a readable pickup reference from shipment values.\n        /// </summary>\n        private string BuildPickupReference(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest) {\n            // Step 1: Read the configured prefix to keep pickup references standardized.\n            string prefix = GetSetting(\"BiologicalReturns.PickupDefaultReferencePrefix\", \"MARKEN\");\n            // Step 2: Use the shipper reference if it exists because it already carries protocol/study data.\n            string shipRef = shipmentRequest != null && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ShipperReference) ? shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ShipperReference : \"UNKNOWN\";\n            // Step 3: Concatenate the prefix and shipment identifier into a stable pickup reference string.\n            return prefix + \"-\" + shipRef;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Reads a business rule setting with a fallback default value.\n        /// </summary>\n        private string GetSetting(string key, string defaultValue) {\n            // Step 1: Use the template Tools helper to find the setting in Commander.\n            Tools tools = new Tools(_logger);\n            // Step 2: Look up the configured value by key.\n            string value = tools.GetStringValueFromBusinessRuleSettings(key, _settings);\n            // Step 3: Return the configured value if present; otherwise return the supplied default.\n            return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) ? defaultValue : value;\n        }\n    }\n}"}

=== CBR ANALYSIS (for context) ===
{"analysis":"The blueprint requires only two active client-side hooks and no other CBR methods. PageLoaded is needed to auto-route users to the shipping page on login and to apply page-level UI behavior such as hiding the Pickup button when the pickup-from country is Canada, hiding/disabling dry ice editing unless Temperature is Frozen, and suppressing the Rate button. NewShipment is needed to seed a fresh returns shipment with user-profile defaults: copy the user address into the Pickup From/Consignee fields, default Shipper Reference and MiscReference fields from Custom2/Custom1/Custom3, set the description, default the biological sample flag, and establish the initial UI/shipping state. PreShip is needed as a client-side convenience step to stage pickup association for non-Canada returns before the request is sent, but the blueprint explicitly treats the server-side SBR PreShip/Ship logic as authoritative fallback, so the CBR version should only attempt the UI save/pickup action and never be the only enforcement point. All other CBR hooks are not required by the blueprint and should remain null.","cbrMethods":{"PageLoaded":"/*\n * PageLoaded hook for the Marken Phase 1 biological returns workflow.\n *\n * Why this exists:\n * - The blueprint requires the user to be automatically routed to the shipping page on login.\n * - It also requires page-level UI interlocks for Pickup visibility, dry ice editability,\n *   and hiding the Rate button on the biological returns shipping template.\n *\n * Blueprint requirements fulfilled:\n * - \"On login, user should automatically be taken to the shipping page.\"\n * - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is set to 'Frozen', have user enter in the required Dry Ice Weight...\"\n * - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is any other value besides 'Frozen', Dry Ice Weight should not be editable\"\n * - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button...\"\n * - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button\"\n * - \"Hide Rate Button\"\n *\n * Program flow:\n * 1. Determine whether we are on the login/home route and redirect to shipping when needed.\n * 2. Evaluate the current consignee/pickup-from country to decide whether Pickup should be visible.\n * 3. Evaluate Temperature (stored in ConsigneeReference) to decide whether dry ice input should be editable.\n * 4. Hide the Rate button because the blueprint says shipping must follow the biological returns workflow.\n * 5. Leave shipment data itself untouched; this hook is strictly for page/UI state.\n *\n * Hook chain relationship:\n * - This hook runs before NewShipment and before the user begins entering a new shipment.\n * - NewShipment will populate default values, while PreShip/SBR PreShip enforce final business rules.\n */\n// Step 1: Preserve the current route value in a safe local variable so we can make page decisions.\nvar currentLocation = (location || \"\").toString().toLowerCase();\n\n// Step 2: If the user is on the landing/login route, send them directly to the shipping screen.\n// This supports the blueprint's requirement that the shipping page becomes the default entry point.\nif (currentLocation === \"\" || currentLocation === \"/\" || currentLocation.indexOf(\"login\") >= 0 || currentLocation.indexOf(\"home\") >= 0) {\n    // Step 2a: Use the browser location when available so the user lands on the shipping workflow immediately.\n    if (typeof window !== \"undefined\" && window.location) {\n        window.location.hash = \"#/shipping\";\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 3: Read the current shipment request from the view model so we can apply UI rules based on live data.\nvar shipmentRequest = this.vm && this.vm.shipmentRequest ? this.vm.shipmentRequest : null;\n\n// Step 4: Safely read the consignee/pickup-from country, because the UI hides the Pickup button for Canada.\nvar pickupCountry = \"\";\nif (shipmentRequest && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country) {\n    // Normalize the country so comparisons are reliable regardless of casing.\n    pickupCountry = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n}\n\n// Step 5: Safely read the temperature value, which the blueprint stores in ConsigneeReference.\nvar temperature = \"\";\nif (shipmentRequest && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeReference) {\n    // Temperature is a validation-list value, so we compare as a trimmed string.\n    temperature = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeReference.toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n}\n\n// Step 6: Hide or show the Pickup button based on whether the pickup-from country is Canada.\n// We prefer the view-model state when the control exists, because template fields are manipulated client-side.\nif (this.vm) {\n    // Step 6a: Ensure a client-side UI state bag exists so we can store visibility flags without reading the DOM.\n    this.vm.uiState = this.vm.uiState || {};\n    // Step 6b: Canada pickups should not show the Pickup button per blueprint requirements.\n    this.vm.uiState.HidePickupButton = (pickupCountry === \"CA\");\n}\n\n// Step 7: Apply dry ice editability rules based on the temperature selection.\nif (this.vm) {\n    // Step 7a: Keep a dedicated state flag for the dry ice editor so the template can disable the control.\n    this.vm.uiState = this.vm.uiState || {};\n    // Step 7b: Dry ice is editable only when the temperature is Frozen.\n    this.vm.uiState.AllowDryIceEdit = (temperature === \"FROZEN\");\n}\n\n// Step 8: The blueprint requires the Rate button to be hidden in the biological returns template.\nif (this.vm) {\n    // Step 8a: Store a UI flag that the template can bind to for hiding the rate action.\n    this.vm.uiState = this.vm.uiState || {};\n    this.vm.uiState.HideRateButton = true;\n}","NewShipment":"/*\n * NewShipment hook for the Marken Phase 1 biological returns workflow.\n *\n * Why this exists:\n * - Every new shipment must start with the user's saved return-label defaults.\n * - The blueprint maps user CustomData to shipment references and uses the user address as the Pickup From address.\n * - This hook is the correct place to seed a blank shipment because it runs whenever ShipExec creates a new shipment shell.\n *\n * Blueprint requirements fulfilled:\n * - \"Set Consignee address to User address values\" (used as Pickup From for returns)\n * - \"Set Shipper Reference/Study Reference Code to User Custom2 value\"\n * - \"Set MiscReference1/Protocol Number to User Custom1 value\"\n * - \"Set MiscReference2/Site Number to User Custom3 value\"\n * - \"MiscReference4/Biological Sample\" defaulted to true/on\n * - \"Description – 'UN3373 Category B Human Sample'\"\n *\n * Program flow:\n * 1. Make sure the request and package defaults exist.\n * 2. Pull the current user's address and custom data from the profile.\n * 3. Copy user address values into the Pickup From/Consignee fields.\n * 4. Map Custom1/Custom2/Custom3 into the reference fields required by the blueprint.\n * 5. Default the biological sample flag and shipment description.\n * 6. Seed UI state so later hooks know whether Pickup and Dry Ice controls should be shown.\n *\n * Hook chain relationship:\n * - PageLoaded may redirect the user here.\n * - NewShipment sets the initial defaults before the user edits anything.\n * - PreShip and SBR PreShip later validate and enforce the final business rules.\n */\n// Step 1: Ensure a shipment object exists before we attempt to write defaults into it.\nif (!shipmentRequest) {\n    shipmentRequest = {};\n}\n\n// Step 2: Ensure PackageDefaults exists because all blueprint defaults are shipment-level values.\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults || {};\n\n// Step 3: Ensure the consignee object exists because the template repurposes it as Pickup From.\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee || {};\n\n// Step 4: Ensure at least one package exists so the shipment can be built and later shipped.\nshipmentRequest.Packages = shipmentRequest.Packages || [{}];\n\n// Step 5: Read the logged-in user's profile address and custom data from the view model.\nvar userInfo = this.vm && this.vm.profile && this.vm.profile.UserInformation ? this.vm.profile.UserInformation : null;\nvar userAddress = userInfo && userInfo.Address ? userInfo.Address : null;\n\n// Step 6: Copy the user's address into the Pickup From/Consignee fields so return labels start from the user location.\nif (userAddress) {\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Company = userAddress.Company || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Company || \"\";\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Contact = userAddress.Contact || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Contact || \"\";\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address1 = userAddress.Address1 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address1 || \"\";\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address2 = userAddress.Address2 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address2 || \"\";\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address3 = userAddress.Address3 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address3 || \"\";\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.City = userAddress.City || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.City || \"\";\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.StateProvince = userAddress.StateProvince || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.StateProvince || \"\";\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.PostalCode = userAddress.PostalCode || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.PostalCode || \"\";\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country = userAddress.Country || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country || \"\";\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Phone = userAddress.Phone || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Phone || \"\";\n}\n\n// Step 7: Define a small helper for reading CustomData values from the user's address.\nfunction readCustom(customKey) {\n    // Step 7a: If no custom data exists, return an empty string so the caller can safely assign defaults.\n    if (!userAddress || !userAddress.CustomData) {\n        return \"\";\n    }\n    // Step 7b: Search the list because CustomData is stored as an array of key/value objects, not a dictionary.\n    for (var i = 0; i < userAddress.CustomData.length; i++) {\n        if (userAddress.CustomData[i] && userAddress.CustomData[i].Key === customKey) {\n            return userAddress.CustomData[i].Value || \"\";\n        }\n    }\n    // Step 7c: No match means the field should remain blank.\n    return \"\";\n}\n\n// Step 8: Map Custom2 into the Shipper Reference / Study Reference Code field.\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ShipperReference = readCustom(\"Custom2\") || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ShipperReference || \"\";\n\n// Step 9: Map Custom1 into MiscReference1 / Protocol Number.\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference1 = readCustom(\"Custom1\") || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference1 || \"\";\n\n// Step 10: Map Custom3 into MiscReference2 / Site Number.\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference2 = readCustom(\"Custom3\") || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference2 || \"\";\n\n// Step 11: Default the biological sample flag to true because the blueprint says it is on by default.\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4 = (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4 === undefined || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4 === null || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4 === \"\") ? true : shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4;\n\n// Step 12: Default the shipment description to the required biological returns text when empty.\nif (!shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Description) {\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Description = \"UN3373 Category B Human Sample\";\n}\n\n// Step 13: Keep the first package aligned with the shipment defaults so later client/server hooks see consistent data.\nshipmentRequest.Packages[0].ShipperReference = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ShipperReference;\nshipmentRequest.Packages[0].MiscReference1 = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference1;\nshipmentRequest.Packages[0].MiscReference2 = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference2;\nshipmentRequest.Packages[0].MiscReference4 = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4;\nshipmentRequest.Packages[0].Description = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Description;\n\n// Step 14: Seed the UI state used by PageLoaded so the template can react immediately after a new shipment is created.\nif (this.vm) {\n    this.vm.uiState = this.vm.uiState || {};\n    // Pickup should be hidden only for Canada, so compute the flag from the newly defaulted consignee country.\n    this.vm.uiState.HidePickupButton = (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country || \"\").toString().trim().toUpperCase() === \"CA\";\n    // Dry ice editing is not allowed unless Temperature is Frozen; initial shipments usually start locked.\n    this.vm.uiState.AllowDryIceEdit = false;\n}","PreShip":"/*\n * PreShip hook for the Marken Phase 1 biological returns workflow.\n *\n * Why this exists:\n * - The blueprint recommends trying to associate the Pickup before the shipment is sent,\n *   especially when the Pickup From country is not Canada.\n * - This is a client-side convenience step only; the server-side SBR PreShip and Ship hooks\n *   remain the authoritative fallback if the pickup save/action fails.\n *\n * Blueprint requirements fulfilled:\n * - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button to associate Pickup with return label\"\n * - \"Recommended... automatically click the 'Pickup Request' button and 'Save'\"\n * - \"If there are issues with this working correctly from the client side, we will need to develop a backup strategy...\"\n *\n * Program flow:\n * 1. Inspect the current shipment and determine whether the pickup-from country is Canada.\n * 2. If the country is not Canada, attempt the client-side pickup action.\n * 3. If the client action is unavailable or fails, do not block shipping here; server-side fallback will handle it.\n * 4. Preserve the shipment object so ShipExec can continue to the server.\n *\n * Hook chain relationship:\n * - NewShipment seeds the default address/reference data.\n * - PageLoaded controls visibility and UI state.\n * - This PreShip hook attempts to stage pickup data before the request is sent.\n * - SBR PreShip/SBR Ship provide the enforceable fallback and final business rules.\n */\n// Step 1: Read the active shipment from the view model so we can decide whether pickup staging is needed.\nvar shipmentRequest = this.vm && this.vm.shipmentRequest ? this.vm.shipmentRequest : null;\n\n// Step 2: If there is no shipment yet, there is nothing to stage, so exit quietly.\nif (!shipmentRequest || !shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults || !shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 3: Normalize the pickup-from country for a reliable Canada comparison.\nvar pickupCountry = (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country || \"\").toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n\n// Step 4: Only attempt the pickup action when the shipment is not going to Canada.\nif (pickupCountry !== \"CA\") {\n    // Step 4a: If a UI state flag says the button should be hidden, still attempt the action only when the control exists.\n    if (this.vm) {\n        this.vm.uiState = this.vm.uiState || {};\n    }\n\n    // Step 4b: Attempt to trigger the client-side pickup workflow in a defensive way.\n    // The exact control name can vary by template, so we check for several common action patterns.\n    var pickupTriggered = false;\n    if (typeof this.vm.clickPickup === \"function\") {\n        this.vm.clickPickup();\n        pickupTriggered = true;\n    } else if (typeof this.vm.Pickup === \"function\") {\n        this.vm.Pickup();\n        pickupTriggered = true;\n    } else if (typeof this.vm.requestPickup === \"function\") {\n        this.vm.requestPickup();\n        pickupTriggered = true;\n    }\n\n    // Step 4c: If we could not find a client-side pickup trigger, we intentionally do nothing else here.\n    // The shipment is still allowed to continue because the server-side SBR Ship hook is the backup path.\n    if (!pickupTriggered) {\n        // No-op by design: the fallback is handled server-side to avoid blocking the user.\n    }\n\n    // Step 4d: Ask the UI to save the current shipment when a save action is exposed by the view model.\n    if (typeof this.vm.save === \"function\") {\n        this.vm.save();\n    } else if (typeof this.vm.Save === \"function\") {\n        this.vm.Save();\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 5: Never block shipping here; return normally so the request can proceed to server validation.\n"} }
📩 Response Received (24,203 chars) 
{"analysis":"The blueprint only requires changes to shippingTemplate.html. The shipping screen must be transformed into Marken’s biological returns workflow by renaming the Consignee tab to Pickup From, relabeling reference fields to Temperature/Study Reference Guide/Protocol Number/Site Number/Dry Ice Weight (kg)/Biological Sample, hiding a defined set of billing/package/dimensions/origin fields, defaulting the description, hiding the Rate button, and adding UI/state support for the Biological Sample and temperature-controlled dry ice behavior. No other templates are explicitly required to change by the blueprint. The client-side logic for page routing, new shipment defaults, temperature-based dry ice editability, and pickup visibility belongs in CBR rather than template markup, so no additional template files are necessary.","templateChanges":[{"file":"shippingTemplate.html","description":"Updated the shipping screen for Marken Phase 1 biological returns: renamed tabs and reference captions, hid specified fields, defaulted description, added Biological Sample and temperature-controlled dry ice behavior, and hid the Rate button / Pickup button per blueprint rules.","fullContent":"<div class=\"container-fluid shipping-template\">\n    <div class=\"row\">\n        <div class=\"col-md-4\">\n            <div class=\"toolbar\">\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.newShipment()\">New(F2)</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.repeatShipment()\">Repeat(F8)</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.buildShipment()\" ng-if=\"vm.buildShipment\">Build</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.showDistributionList = !vm.showDistributionList\" ng-if=\"vm.showDistributionList !== undefined\">DistList</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.pickup()\" ng-hide=\"vm.uiState && vm.uiState.HidePickupButton\">Pickup</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.loadPending()\" ng-if=\"!vm.profile.ProfileSetting.DisablePendingShipment\">Pending</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.mailRoom()\" ng-if=\"vm.profile.ProfileSetting.EnableMailRoomProcessing\">MailRoom</button>\n            </div>\n\n            <div class=\"form-horizontal\">\n                <field-select property=\"LoadFrom\"\n                              model=\"vm.loadFrom\"\n                              options=\"item.Value as item.Name for item in vm.loadFromOptions\"\n                              caption=\"Load From\"\n                              change-function=\"vm.loadFromChanged()\"\n                              form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                </field-select>\n\n                <field-input property=\"Load\"\n                             model=\"vm.loadValue\"\n                             caption=\"Load\"\n                             form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                             ng-if=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.Load.IsHidden\">\n                </field-input>\n\n                <field-select property=\"Shipper\"\n                              model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Shipper\"\n                              options=\"item.Symbol as item.Name for item in vm.profile.Shippers\"\n                              caption=\"Shipper\"\n                              change-function=\"vm.shipperChanged()\"\n                              form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                              ng-if=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.Shipper.IsHidden\">\n                </field-select>\n\n                <field-date property=\"ShipDate\"\n                            model=\"vm.startshipdate.value\"\n                            caption=\"Ship Date\"\n                            is-open=\"vm.startshipdate.opened\"\n                            datepicker-options=\"vm.dateOptions\"\n                            change-function=\"vm.shipDateChanged()\"\n                            form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                            ng-hide=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions.ShipDate.IsHidden\">\n                </field-date>\n\n                <field-select property=\"Terms\"\n                              model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Terms\"\n                              options=\"item.Value as item.Name for item in vm.profile.PaymentTerms\"\n                              caption=\"Terms\"\n                              form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                              ng-if=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.Terms.IsHidden\">\n                </field-select>\n\n                <field-checkbox property=\"ThirdPartyBilling\"\n                                model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ThirdPartyBilling\"\n                                caption=\"3rd Party\"\n                                change=\"vm.thirdPartyBillingChanged()\"\n                                ng-hide=\"true\">\n                </field-checkbox>\n\n                <field-checkbox property=\"BillConsignee\"\n                                model=\"vm.billConsignee\"\n                                caption=\"Bill Consignee\"\n                                ng-hide=\"true\">\n                </field-checkbox>\n\n                <field-checkbox property=\"Brokerage\"\n                                model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BrokerageThirdPartyBilling\"\n                                caption=\"Brokerage\"\n                                ng-hide=\"true\">\n                </field-checkbox>\n            </div>\n\n            <ul class=\"nav nav-tabs address-tabs\">\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 0}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.Consignee.IsHidden\">\n                    <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 0\">Pickup From</a>\n                </li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 1}\" ng-show=\"vm.showDistributionList\">\n                    <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 1\">Distribution List</a>\n                </li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 2}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.ReturnAddress.IsHidden\">\n                    <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 2\">Return Address</a>\n                </li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 3}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.OriginAddress.IsHidden\">\n                    <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 3\">Origin</a>\n                </li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 4}\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ThirdPartyBilling\">\n                    <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 4\">3rd Party Addr</a>\n                </li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 5}\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeThirdPartyBilling\">\n                    <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 5\">Consignee 3rd</a>\n                </li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 6}\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BrokerageThirdPartyBilling\">\n                    <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 6\">Brokerage Addr</a>\n                </li>\n            </ul>\n\n            <div class=\"address-tab-content\">\n                <div ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 0 && !vm.profile.FieldOptions.Consignee.IsHidden\">\n                    <name-address caption=\"Pickup From\"\n                                  nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Consignee\"\n                                  countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                  translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                  field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                  address-property-name=\"Consignee\"\n                                  current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                  hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                  hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                  hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                  hide-search=\"false\"\n                                  active-subtab-index=\"0\"\n                                  is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                  address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                  disable-Fields=\"false\"\n                                  hide-po-box=\"true\"\n                                  ng-if=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.Consignee.IsHidden\">\n                    </name-address>\n                </div>\n\n                <div ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 2 && !vm.profile.FieldOptions.ReturnAddress.IsHidden\">\n                    <name-address caption=\"Return Address\"\n                                  nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ReturnAddress\"\n                                  countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                  translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                  field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n        active-subtab-index=\"2\"\n                                  is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 3 && !vm.profile.FieldOptions.OriginAddress.IsHidden\">\n                    <name-address caption=\"Origin\"\n                                  nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.OriginAddress\"\n                                  countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                  translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                  field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                  address-property-name=\"OriginAddress\"\n <name-address caption=\"3rd Party Addr\"\n                                  nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 5 && vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeThirdPartyBilling\">\n                    <name-address caption=\"Consignee 3rd\"\n                                  nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                  countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n hide-search=\"false\"\n                                  active-subtab-index=\"5\"\n                                  is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                  address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                  disable-Fields=\"false\">\n                    </name-address>\n                </div>\n\n                <div ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 6 && vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BrokerageThirdPartyBilling\">\n                    <name-address caption=\"Brokerage Addr\"\n                                  nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BrokerageThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                  countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                  translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                  field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                  address-property-name=\"BrokerageThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                  current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                  hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                  hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                  hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                  hide-search=\"false\"\n                                  active-subtab-index=\"6\"\n                                  is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                  address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                  disable-Fields=\"false\">\n                    </name-address>\n                </div>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n\n        <div class=\"col-md-8\">\n            <ul class=\"nav nav-tabs\">\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeTab === 0}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.General.IsHidden\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 0\">General</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeTab === 1}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.Accessorials.IsHidden\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 1\">Accessorials</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeTab === 2}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.Intl.IsHidden\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 2\">Intl</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeTab === 3}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.Hazmat.IsHidden\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 3\">Hazmat</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeTab === 4}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.Reference.IsHidden\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 4\">Reference</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeTab === 5}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.LTL.IsHidden\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 5\">LTL</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeTab === 6}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.Visibility.IsHidden\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 6\">Visibility</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeTab === 7}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.Goods.IsHidden\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 7\">Goods</a></li>\n            </ul>\n\n            <div class=\"tab-content panel panel-default\">\n                <div class=\"panel-body\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 0 && !vm.profile.FieldOptions.General.IsHidden\">\n                    <general-tab shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" profile=\"vm.profile\"></general-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"panel-body\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 1 && !vm.profile.FieldOptions.Accessorials.IsHidden\">\n                    <accessorial-tab shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" profile=\"vm.profile\"></accessorial-tab>\n                    <div class=\"row\">\n                        <div class=\"col-md-12\">\n                            <field-checkbox property=\"SaturdayDelivery\"\n                                            model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.SaturdayDelivery\"\n                                            caption=\"Saturday Delivery\"\n                                            change=\"vm.saturdayDeliveryChanged()\">\n                            </field-checkbox>\n                            <field-checkbox property=\"BiologicalSample\"\n                                            model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4\"\n                                            caption=\"Biological Sample\"\n                                            change=\"vm.biologicalSampleChanged()\"\n                                            ng-if=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.MiscReference4.IsHidden\">\n                            </field-checkbox>\n                        </div>\n                    </div>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"panel-body\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 2 && !vm.profile.FieldOptions.Intl.IsHidden\">\n                    <international-tab shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" profile=\"vm.profile\"></international-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"panel-body\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 3 && !vm.profile.FieldOptions.Hazmat.IsHidden\">\n                    <hazmat-tab shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" profile=\"vm.profile\"></hazmat-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"panel-body\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 4 && !vm.profile.FieldOptions.Reference.IsHidden\">\n                    <reference-tab shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" profile=\"vm.profile\"></reference-tab>\n\n                    <div class=\"form-horizontal\">\n                        <field-input property=\"ShipperReference\"\n                                     model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ShipperReference\"\n                                     caption=\"Study Reference Guide\"\n                                     form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                                     ng-hide=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions.ShipperReference.IsHidden\">\n                        </field-input>\n\n                        <field-select property=\"ConsigneeReference\"\n                                      model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeReference\"\n                                      options=\"item.Value as item.Name for item in vm.temperatureOptions\"\n                                      caption=\"Temperature\"\n                                      change-function=\"vm.temperatureChanged()\"\n                                      form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                                      ng-hide=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions.ConsigneeReference.IsHidden\">\n                        </field-select>\n\n                        <field-input property=\"MiscReference1\"\n                                     model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.MiscReference1\"\n                                     caption=\"Protocol Number\"\n
